drug_details.pack_description:
Finished: Carton box containing 4 strips of 5 translucent polyethylene plastic vials each of 1ml solution, each strip is over wrapped in aluminum pouch and insert leaflet.
Active: LDPE bags placed in HDPE container. First the material is packed in the inner transparent food grade LDPE antistatic bag and twist tied using plastic fastener. Then is placed in one transparent and two black color LDPE antistatic bags and all LDPE bags are twist tied using plastic fasteners individually. One product label is pasted on the outer black LDPE bag. Then HDPE container is closed with air tight lid and locked with clamp. Then the clamp is sealed with PP seal (male& female locking system) which has non repeatable number. The other product label is affixed on the container. The entire container is shrink wrapped.

registration_details.physical_characters: White to almost white powder giving clear to pale opalescent, colorless to pale yellow solution after reconstitution.
registration_details.storage_conditions:
Don’t store above 25°C, don’t refrigerate or freeze.
The only approved solvent for preparation of sterile gemcitabine powder is sodium chloride 9 mg/ ml (0.9%) solution for injection (without preservative).
For reconstitution, add 5 ml sodium chloride 9 mg/ml (0.9%).
Chemical and physical stability was demonstrated for 24 hours at 25°C.
For Microbiological reasons, the solution should be used immediately, the user is responsible for retention time and conditions. This should normally not be more than 24 hours at room temperature, unless the solution and further dilution (if applicable) were carried out under validated aseptic conditions. The prepared Gemcitabine solution must not be kept refrigerated as precipitation may occur.

The prepared solution may 
be either administrated directly or diluted with 100 ml of sodium chloride 9mg/ml( 0.9%) or 55mg/ml(5%) glucose injectable solution.
Both reconstituted and diluted solution can be stored for 24 hrs at room temperature.
From microbiological point of view: the product should be used immediately,
if not used immediately  in use storage times and conditions are the responsibility of the user.
Both reconstituted and admixed solution don’t need to be protected from light.

registration_details.physical_characters: clear colourless solution free from visible particulate matter.
registration_details.storage_conditions:
Store at temperature not exceeding 25 °,Protect from light 
after dilution:
chemical & Physical in use , stability of the solution after dilution in glucose 5% for 96 hours at (2-8°C) & (20°C to 25 °C).
 chemical & Physical in use , stability of the solution after dilution in Sodium chloride 9% for 24 hours at (2-8°C) & 8 hours at (20°C to 25 °C).
From Microbiological point of view :
- the diluted product should be used immediately .
- if not used immediately , in use storage times & conditions prior to use are the responsibility of the user & would normally not be longer than 24 hours at 2 °C to 8 °C unless dilution has taken place in controlled & validated aseptic conditions.
Precaution :
Carboplatin may interact with aluminum to form a black precipitate.
 needles ,syringes , catheters or IV administration sets containing  aluminum parts that may come into contact with carboplatin should not be used for preparation or administration of carboplatin.

Store at temperature not exceeding 30 °C for 30 minutes

registration_details.physical_characters: Clear, yellow solution free of particles
registration_details.storage_conditions:
Don't Store above 25°C, Protect from light.
Precautions: 
The stability of the following methotrexate solutions has been tested under light protection, at 25 °C (room temperature) over 24 hours:
5% glucose solution
0.9% Sodium chloride solution
The Chemical and physical stability of the ready for use preparation was demonstrated for 28 days at 2 °C to 8 °C  and at room temperature(20-25 °C ) with protection from light
From microbiological viewpoint, the ready to use preparation to be used immediately. If the ready to used preparation is not used immediately, the user is responsible for the duration and condition of storage. If the ready to use solution is not prepared under controlled and validated aseptic conditions, it must not be stored for longer than 24 hours at 2 °C to 8 °C
-For single use only discard any surplus
-The solution must be visually inspected first before use
-Only clear solutions, which practically free of particles should be used
Known incompatibilities: 
-Strong oxidants, strong acid, chlorpromazine HCl, droperidol, idarubicin, metoclopramide HCl, Prednisolone sodium phosphate, Promethazine HCl
-Incompatibilities of methotrexate with Cytarabine and flurouracil have been reported, however the incompatibility with fluorouracil is in doubt
-Incompatibilities with other substances may occur if the solution is stored for more than 24 hours

drug_details.pack_description:
For finished drug product: Carton box containing 1, 2 or 3 (Al. foil / opaque triplex (PVC/PE/PVDC) strips each contains 10 film coated tablets with inner leaflet.
For active drug substance: Product is packed in LDPE clear, colorless polyethylene bag, which is sealed with irreversible yellow plastic strip seal. This bag is placed in outer clear colorless LDPE polyethylene bag containing desiccant (Dried silica bag) and the inner product label is kept in between the two polyethylene bags, which contains necessary information with respect to Batch no., Date of manufacturing, Date of expiry, Net weight, etc. This outer bag (middle bag) is sealed with irreversible yellow plastic strip seal. The sealed bags are then placed in HDPE drums. The HDPE container is closed with a lid sealed using a Precise Logo tamper proof, metal seal having Unique Code No. Product label affixed on outer side of the HDPE drum.

White to slightly yellow hygroscopic powder

registration_details.storage_conditions:
Store at temperature not exceeding 25°C protected from light.

After dilution:
Store at temperature not exceeding 25°C for 24 hours using:
5% Dextrose Injection, 5% Dextrose + Lactated Ringer’s Injection, 5% Dextrose + 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection, 5% Dextrose + 0.2% Sodium Chloride Injection, 10% Dextrose Injection, Lactated Ringer’s Injection, 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection, Normosol® M + 5% Dextrose Injection and Normosol® R.
Or store at temperature (2-8°C) for 5 days using:
5% Dextrose Injection, 5% Dextrose + 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection, 5% Dextrose + 0.2% Sodium Chloride Injection, Lactated Ringer’s Injection, 0.9% Sodium Chloride Injection, Normosol® M + 5% Dextrose Injection and Normosol® R.

registration_details.storage_conditions:
Don't store above 30 °C 
-Reconstituted solution:Do not use paraben-containing bacteriostatic water for injection ,since it is incompatible with the sterile lyophilised active substance of Cymevene & may cause precipitation ,the reconstituted solution is stable at room temperature (15-20 )°C for 12 hours ,Do not store in a refrigerator.

drug_details.dosage_form: Powder for Solution for I.V Infusion
drug_details.pack_description: Carton box contains one colorless (type I) glass vial of 20ml with safety cap with insert leaflet.

registration_details.physical_characters:
Before reconstitution: White to light yellow powder
After reconstitution: Solution is clear colorless to yellow
registration_details.storage_conditions:
Store at temperature not exceeding 25°C. Don’t freeze the reconstituted solution.
Precaution:
-Contents of each vial must be transferred to 100ml of an appropriate infusion solution, 0.9% sodium chloride, in exceptional circumstances where 0.9% sodium chloride cannot be used for clinical reasons 5% Glucose may be used instead.
-A suggested procedure is to add approximately 10ml of the appropriate infusion solution to the vial.
-Shake well and transfer the resulting mixture to the infusion solution container.
-Caution: the mixture is not for direct infusion.
-Repeat with an additional 10ml of infusion solution to ensure complete transfer of vial contents to the infusion solution. The resulting mixture should be agitated until clear.
-The concentration of the reconstituted solution following the above procedure is approximately 5mg/ml for both Imipenem and Cilastatin.
-Variations of color from colorless to yellow do not affect the potency of the product.

registration_details.storage_conditions:
Don’t store above 30 °C, Do not freeze and keep the vial in outer carton to protect from light.
After Opening prior to dilution: the concentrate should be used immediately
After Dilution in 5% glucose: The chemical and physical in-use stability of the diluted solution is demonstrated for 24 hours if stored at 2 – 8 °C and stable for 6 hours if stored at 25°C
From a microbiological point of view, the diluted solution should be used immediately 
If not used immediately, in-use storage and conditions prior to use are the responsibility of the user and would normally not be longer than 24 hours at 2 °C to 8 °C unless dilution has taken place in controlled and validated aseptic conditions

drug_details.pack_description: 1500 ml or 2000 ml three compartment multilayer plastic bag. The inner (contact) layer of the bag material is made of a blend of polyolefinic copolymers and is compatible with amino acid solutions, glucose solution and lipid emulsions. Other layers are made of polyethylene vinyl acetate (EVA), and of copolyester. The glucose compartment if fitted with an injection site to be used for addition of supplements. The amino acid compartment is fitted with an administration site for insertion of the spike of the infusion set. The bag is packaged in an oxygen barrier overpouch with an oxygen absorber sachet with insert leaflet.

After first opening the medicinal product should be used immediately.
For infusion:
Chemical& physical in us stability has been demonstrated for 48 hours at 25°C using the following diluents:
•	Sodium chloride 9mg/ml (0.9% w/v) Solution.
•	Glucose 50mg/ml (5% w/v) Solution.
•	Mannitol 100mg/ml (10% w/v) Solution.
•	Ringer lactated solution.
From microbiological point of view, the diluted solution should be used immediately, if not used immediately in use storage and conditions are the responsibility of the user and would be normally no longer than 24 hours at 2-8°C, unless dilution has taken place in controlled and validated aseptic conditions.
The diluted solution should be protected from light.

registration_details.storage_conditions:
Store at temperature between 20- 25 °C(68°F-77°F), which excursions permitted between 15-30°C (59°F  -86°F),Dispense in tight and light resistant container.
Tablets don’t need to be refrigerated.
Store in the container that it comes in and keep the container tightly closed. 
Keep tablets and NITYR that has been prepared in an oral syringe with water or in applesauce out of direct sunlight. 
Take NITYR within 24 hours of adding water to NITYR tablets in an oral syringe.
Throw away an unused NITYR tablets and water stored in the oral syringe after 24 hours of being mixed together.
Take NITYR within 2 of adding crushed NITYR tablets to applesauce
Throw away any crushed NITYR tablets and applesauce mixture that has not been used within 2 hours.
